title: Student Progress Report Writer
promptText:
You are an experienced teacher who writes thoughtful, individualized student progress reports that are both honest and encouraging. Write a progress report for the following student:

Student First Name: [STUDENT_NAME]
Grade Level: [GRADE_LEVEL]
Subject: [SUBJECT]
Reporting Period: [REPORTING_PERIOD]
Current Grade/Performance Level: [PERFORMANCE_LEVEL]
Academic Strengths: [STRENGTHS]
Areas for Growth: [GROWTH_AREAS]
Behavior/Social Notes: [BEHAVIOR_NOTES]
Specific Examples: [SPECIFIC_EXAMPLES]
Goals for Next Period: [GOALS]

The progress report should:
- Open with a genuine positive observation about the student
- Describe academic performance with specific examples rather than generic statements
- Frame areas for growth constructively — focus on next steps rather than deficiencies
- Include at least one specific anecdote or classroom moment that shows you know this student as an individual
- Provide 2-3 actionable recommendations for parents to support learning at home
- Close with an encouraging statement about the student's potential
- Use professional, warm language appropriate for parent communication
- Avoid educational jargon that parents may not understand

Keep the report to 200-250 words. Write in third person using the student's first name.
description: This prompt helps teachers write individualized progress reports that go beyond generic comments like 'a pleasure to have in class' to provide specific, meaningful feedback that parents and students actually find useful. Writing personalized reports for 25-30 students is one of the most time-consuming tasks teachers face each marking period, and this prompt dramatically accelerates the process while maintaining the thoughtful, individual touch that parents expect. The output balances honest assessment with constructive framing, includes specific examples and anecdotes, and provides actionable home support recommendations. It is ideal for teachers during report card season, special education teachers writing IEP progress updates, and administrators who want to raise the quality of school-wide progress communications.

variables.name: STRENGTHS
variables.example: Strong mental math skills, excellent participation in class discussions, helps peers understand concepts

variables.name: GROWTH_AREAS
variables.example: Showing work on multi-step word problems, completing homework consistently, test anxiety affects performance

variables.name: BEHAVIOR_NOTES
variables.example: Natural leader in group work, sometimes talks during independent work time

variables.name: SPECIFIC_EXAMPLES
variables.description: Concrete classroom moments or work samples
variables.example: Solved a challenge problem on the board that stumped the class, but left 3 word problems blank on the last test

variables.name: GOALS
variables.description: Targets for the next reporting period
variables.example: Show all work on word problems, complete 90% of homework assignments, practice test-taking strategies

tips: Jot down one specific anecdote per student throughout the marking period so you have material ready at report time
Be specific in the GROWTH_AREAS field — vague inputs produce vague reports
Review the output to ensure it accurately reflects each student before sending to parents
exampleOutput:
Jaylen continues to impress with his natural mathematical reasoning and ability to solve complex problems mentally — during our fractions unit, he volunteered to explain his strategy for comparing unlike denominators at the board, and his explanation helped several classmates grasp the concept. His enthusiasm for math is contagious, and he is often the first to offer help to peers during group work.

While Jaylen's conceptual understanding is strong, showing his work on multi-step word problems remains an important area of growth. On recent assessments, he arrived at correct answers but could not receive full credit because the problem-solving process was not documented...
metaTitle: Student Progress Report Writer Prompt for ChatGPT
metaDescription: Write individualized student progress reports in minutes with this free ChatGPT prompt. Get specific, encouraging parent communication.
